texto: I do not intend to defend capitalism or capitalists. They, like everything human, have their defects. I only say their possibilities of usefulness are not ended.Capitalism has borne the monstrous burden of the war and today still has the strength to shoulder the burdens of peace. ... It is not simply and solely an accumulation of wealth, it is an elaboration, a selection, a co-ordination of values which is the work of centuries. ... Many think, and I myself am one of them, that capitalism is scarcely at the beginning of its story.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Benito Mussolini: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I do not intend to defend capitalism or capitalists. They, like everything human, have their defe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, capitalism, economics pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e trabalho.

frase_original: I do not intend to defend capitalism or capitalists. They, like everything human, have their defects. I only say their possibilities of usefulness are not ended.Capitalism has borne the monstrous burden of the war and today still has the strength to shoulder the burdens of peace. ... It is not simply and solely an accumulation of wealth, it is an elaboration, a selection, a co-ordination of values which is the work of centuries. ... Many think, and I myself am one of them, that capitalism is scarcely at the beginning of its story.
